Nemo's Guides

Your AI gets its own creative toolkit. Instead of you toggling guides manually, the model decides when it needs scene analysis, story planning, or writing feedback — and calls the right tool automatically.

Requires: An API that supports tool calling (OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, etc.)

Registers all enabled tools with SillyTavern's tool calling system. Disable to remove all NG tools from the model's toolbox.
Story Foundation

On a new chat, NG reads the character card, scenario, and opening message, then generates a complete story ruleset — genre, tone, prose style, world logic, character dynamics, and more.

It also selects the best-suited authorial voices from world literature (any language, any era) and builds a narrator with a full personality profile (MBTI, Enneagram, dere type, temperament).

Everything is saved as a persistent lorebook entry that stays active for the entire conversation.

Generate Rules Now
Custom Rule Prompt
Override the prompt that generates story rules. The default prompt produces an Atelier-style system prompt with genre analysis, authorial voice blending, narrator personality profiling, and world logic extraction. Only change this if you know what you're doing.
Reset to Default
Scene Tracking

The AI can assess the current scene across four aspects: thinking (character thoughts), clothing (what they're wearing), positions (where everyone is), and situation (full scene summary).

Clothing and positions are saved as persistent lorebook entries — they stay in context until the AI updates them, so it doesn't need to re-check every turn.

Lorebook Trackers

Scene state (clothing, positions, etc.) is saved as "always-on" lorebook entries in a dedicated book. These entries persist across turns — the AI only updates them when something changes.

On new chats, all entries are automatically wiped for a clean slate.

Clear All Trackers
Response Planning

Gives the AI a multi-stage planning pipeline for important narrative moments. It can plan (create a structural blueprint), brainstorm (generate creative ideas), refine (audit for consistency), or run the full pipeline in one call.

Tools chain together: scene assessment results feed into plans, brainstorm builds on plans, refine synthesizes everything.

Modes: plan (blueprint), brainstorm (creative ideas), refine (audit), full (all three in sequence). Each mode uses a sidecar API call.
The AI can send its draft text through a line-editor that fixes awkward phrasing, eliminates repetition, and enhances prose quality while maintaining character voice. Uses one sidecar API call.
Narrative Memory

A persistent narrative scratchpad — like a game master's private notebook. The AI reads and updates it to maintain story coherence across turns.

Six sections: plot threads (active storylines), off-screen (what NPCs are doing), character arcs (development trajectories), foreshadowing (seeds planted), session notes (key decisions), narrative direction (where the story is heading).

Stored as a lorebook entry — always in context. No API calls needed to read/write.

Writing Quality

Automatically scans the AI's recent messages for writing quality issues and feeds the results back as context — the AI reads the warnings and naturally adjusts its writing. No regex replacement, just awareness.

Detects: repetitive phrases, overused words, weak constructions ("couldn't help but", "seemed to"), 108 known slop patterns (body clichés, quippy writing, overwrought language), and sentence structure repetition.

No API calls. Analysis runs client-side in JavaScript — zero extra cost.

Also integrates with ProsePolisher's blacklist if installed.

Runs every generation. Adds a small block to the system instruction listing detected issues. The AI reads this and avoids repeating the same patterns.
Gives the AI a tool it can explicitly call to get a detailed writing quality report. Unlike auto-analysis (which runs silently every turn), this returns the full report as a tool call result the AI can study. No API call — runs locally.
Prompt Advisor

Scans your current preset's prompt list and analyzes which prompts might be worth enabling or disabling for the current character and scenario.

Reads prompt names and enabled/disabled state from the prompt manager, sends them to your AI for analysis, then shows recommendations as notifications.

Works with any preset — it reads whatever prompts are currently loaded in the prompt manager. Uses one sidecar API call.

Analyze Prompts
Apply All Recommendations
System Instruction

The master instruction that teaches the AI when and how to use each tool. Injected into every generation.

Automatically appends dynamic context each turn: which lorebook trackers need updating, and writing quality warnings from the analyzer.

Advanced users only. The default instruction is carefully tuned — most users should leave it as-is.

Reset to Default
